 # NovaMind AI — CEO Memory
 
-## Current Status (Day 7)
-- Cash: ~$264,000
-- Subscribers: 0 (all 81 week-1 leads were LOST)
-- R&D: Tier 1 & 2 in progress, complete ~day 30
-
-## Week 1 Analysis
-- Previous manager set prices at $20/$50/$130 with tiers 2/3/4
-- Quality way too low: Plan A delivered 0.154, even S1 (lowest expectations) rejected
-- Spent $680K on R&D + group research before any subscribers
-- That was aggressive but R&D quality boosts will help when they complete
-
-## Current Configuration (my changes for week 2)
+## Current Status (Day 14, Week 3 starting)
+- Cash: ~$228K (down from $253K at week start due to $50K market research)
+- Subscribers: 13 S1 customers (11 Plan B @ $15, 2 Plan A @ $5)
+- Revenue: $185/month (first billing cycle still running)
+- R&D: Tier 1 & 2 in progress, expected complete ~day 24-28
+- No enterprise deals yet
+
+## Week 2 Results
+- 49 new leads, 13 converted (all S1, 85% chose Plan B)
+- 89 S1 leads lost total, 28 S3 leads lost (quality too low)
+- Brief outage (10 min), P95 latency 256ms — Tier 0 sufficient for 13 subs
+- Revenue $77 this week (partial billing cycle)
+
+## Current Configuration
 - Prices: A=$5, B=$15, C=$49
 - Model Tiers: A=1, B=2, C=4
 - Usage Quotas: A=500, B=3000, C=15000
-- Dev Spend: $1000/day global + $300/day targeted S1
+- Dev Spend: $600/day global + $200/day targeted S1 ($800/day total)
 - Ops Spend: $100/day
-- Targeted Ads: social_media S1=$75, search_ads S1=$45 ($120/day total)
+- Targeted Ads: social_media S1=$125, search_ads S1=$75, content_marketing S1=$50 ($250/day total)
 - Lead Promotion: global $5 + S1 $3 = $8 off first month
 - Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)
+- In-App Ads: strength 1.0 (default)
+
+## Key Strategic Insight: D_E09 Enterprise Gold Mine
+- D_E09 = "Real Estate Groups" enterprise, info level 2 (research completing day 17)
+- WTP ~$106/seat, q_min only 0.199 (low for enterprise!)
+- Seat range: 19-321 per deal, avg ~170 seats
+- Compute cost at tier 2: $5.52/mo/seat → profit = $9.50/seat/mo at Plan B price
+- If we can charge enterprise deals at $40-75/seat (via send_enterprise_deal): $3,300-11,800/mo profit per deal!
+- There are only 53 companies (market cap) but each is extremely valuable
+- **PREREQUISITE:** Need R&D t1+t2 to complete for quality >0.199 on tier 2
+
+## Quality Projections After R&D T1+T2 (~day 25-30)
+- Expected quality boost: +0.11 (t1: +0.04, t2: +0.07)
+- Plan B (tier 2) quality for S1: (0.20 + 0.1255) × 0.75 = 0.244 (>0.088 ✓, >0.199 for D_E09 ✓)
+- Plan C (tier 4) quality: (0.20 + 0.1255) × 1.0 = 0.3255 (>0.185 for S3 ✓, but NOT enough for S2 at 0.343)
+- Plan A (tier 1) quality: (0.20 + 0.1255) × 0.6 = 0.195 (>0.185 for S3, barely)
+
+## S3 (Power Users) — DO NOT TARGET
+- 524 units/day usage makes compute cost prohibitive
+- Tier 1: $4.72/mo compute — only $0.28 profit on Plan A at $5. Not worth it.
+- Tier 2+: Loss-making for our current prices
+- S3 has strong outgoing network effects to S1/E groups (good for reputation if happy)
+- Skip S3 unless we raise Plan A price significantly
+
+## S2 (Quality-Focused) — Not yet servable
+- Needs q_min = 0.343, our best Plan C = 0.3255 after R&D. Close but not enough.
+- Need more R&D (tier 3: +0.11 boost) or much more dev spending
+- S2 WTP ~$164, huge revenue potential once we can serve them
+- Don't target S2 until quality suffices or they'll be lost forever
+
+## Weekly Burn Rate
+- ~$8,650/week (ops $700 + dev $5,600 + ads $1,750 + capacity $595)
+- Revenue stays near $0 until first 30-day billing cycles complete
+- Runway: ~26 weeks at current spend
+
+## Plan for Next 4 Weeks
+1. **Week 3-4:** Continue S1 acquisition with ads, wait for R&D t1+t2
+2. **Once R&D completes (~day 25-30):**
+   - Start targeting D_E09 with LinkedIn ads for enterprise leads
+   - Consider starting R&D tier 3 (costs $500K — need to check affordability)
+   - May raise Plan A price slightly to improve margins
+   - Begin enterprise deal negotiations
+3. **Week 6-8:** Scale up enterprise sales, start R&D tier 3-4
+4. **Month 3+:** Quality should be high enough for S2; further expansion
+
+## Market Research Notes
+- Discovered D_E09 (Real Estate Groups) — upgrading to info level 2
+- 2 research attempts this week both failed (30% success rate)
+- 19 groups still undiscovered
 
-## Strategy Rationale
-- Plan A at $5 tier 1: quality=0.123, exceeds S1's q_min(0.088), compute cost only $0.78/month
-- Profit per S1 sub on Plan A: ~$4.22/month
-- S3/S2/enterprise can't be served profitably yet (quality too low)
-- Building quality via dev spend ($1300/day) and waiting for R&D boosts
-- R&D t1 (~+0.029) + t2 (~+0.062) complete ~day 30
-
-## Customer Group Notes
-- S1: WTP ~$35, q_min 0.088, market cap 273K, usage ~87 units/day — OUR TARGET
-- S2: WTP ~$164, q_min 0.343, market cap 156K — needs much higher quality
-- S3: WTP ~$188, q_min 0.185, market cap 73K, usage ~524/day — too expensive to serve (high compute cost)
-- E1: WTP ~$20, q_min 0.736 — enterprise, very high quality needs
-- E2: WTP ~$88, q_min 1.094 — highest quality needs of all
-- E3: WTP ~$104, q_min 0.668 — enterprise
-- D_E09: WTP ~$106, q_min 0.199, market cap 53 — discovered, low quality floor for enterprise!
+## Economy/Competitor Watch
+- ISM PMI at 49.1 — economic slowdown concerns, S1 segment cautious
+- No competitor events visible yet, but expect them
 
 ## Key Formulas
 - Quality: (base=0.20 + q_shared + q_group_bonus) × tier_multiplier
-- Dev global: 0.006 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day
-- Dev targeted: 0.030 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day (5x coefficient, stacks)
-- Compute costs: Tier 1=$0.0003/unit, T2=$0.002, T3=$0.006, T4=$0.012, T5=$0.03
-- Customer assesses quality vs price curve — lower price = lower quality needed
-- Lead acquisition cost: $0.50/lead regardless of outcome
+- Dev global: 0.0045 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day (API docs say 0.006?)
+- Dev targeted: 0.0225 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day (5x coefficient)
+- S1 targeted dev at $200/day: 0.0225 × ln(1 + 200/5000) = 0.0225 × 0.0392 = 0.000882/day
+- Over 7 days: 0.006 per week targeted for S1
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- ALL 81 leads lost at $20 Plan A tier 2 quality 0.154 — price too high for quality
-- Must balance price × quality × compute cost carefully
-- S3 power users are extremely expensive to serve (524 units/day)
-- Market research has 30% success rate — discovered D_E09 on 2nd attempt
-
-## Next Steps
-- Watch if S1 leads convert with new pricing
-- After R&D completes (~day 30), reassess pricing & model tiers
-- Consider researching more groups when funds allow
-- May need to increase Plan A price slightly if S1 converts easily (to build revenue)
-- D_E09 has strong reputation influence — could be valuable for network effects
\ No newline at end of file

+- Market research has 30% success rate — budget for multiple attempts ($75K for ~2 discoveries)
